title: HVAC Duct Pressure & Velocity Monitoring
app: Splunk Edge Hub (optional pressure sensor), Modbus TCP integration
spl:
| mstats avg(pressure) as static_press by duct_zone, _time span=10m
| delta static_press as press_delta
| eval filter_condition=case(static_press > 2.5, "CLOGGED", static_press > 1.5, "RESTRICTED", 1=1, "NORMAL")
| stats avg(filter_condition) as predominant_condition, avg(static_press) as avg_press by duct_zone
| where predominant_condition!="NORMAL"
description: Monitors HVAC filter clogging and airflow efficiency by tracking duct static pressure trends.
value: Monitors HVAC filter clogging and airflow efficiency by tracking duct static pressure trends.
implementation: Install Edge Hub pressure sensor in return air duct upstream of main filter. Configure 10-minute sampling. Correlate with Modbus TCP fan speed register reads (port 502) from HVAC controller: increasing pressure + constant fan speed = clogged filter. Typical clogged filter threshold: > 2.5 in H2O (84.7 hPa). Store local SQLite data for 7-day history to track pressure rise rate (rate of clogging). Integrate with OPC-UA SCADA (port 4840) for automated filter change alerts.
visualization: Duct pressure trend, filter condition gauge, maintenance alert timeline.
knownFalsePositives: AQI spikes during cooking, cleaning, paint or solvent use, or HVAC maintenance.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Edge Hub (optional pressure sensor), Modbus TCP integration.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=edge-hub-data` metric_name=pressure, `index=edge-hub-logs` sourcetype=edge_hub modbus_register.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Install Edge Hub pressure sensor in return air duct upstream of main filter. Configure 10-minute sampling. Correlate with Modbus TCP fan speed register reads (port 502) from HVAC controller: increasing pressure + constant fan speed = clogged filter. Typical clogged filter threshold: > 2.5 in H2O (84.7 hPa). Store local SQLite data for 7-day history to track pressure rise rate (rate of clogging). Integrate with OPC-UA SCADA (port 4840) for automated filter change alerts.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
| mstats avg(pressure) as static_press by duct_zone, _time span=10m
| delta static_press as press_delta
| eval filter_condition=case(static_press > 2.5, "CLOGGED", static_press > 1.5, "RESTRICTED", 1=1, "NORMAL")
| stats avg(filter_condition) as predominant_condition, avg(static_press) as avg_press by duct_zone
| where predominant_condition!="NORMAL"
```

Understanding this SPL

**HVAC Duct Pressure & Velocity Monitoring** — Monitors HVAC filter clogging and airflow efficiency by tracking duct static pressure trends.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=edge-hub-data` metric_name=pressure, `index=edge-hub-logs` sourcetype=edge_hub modbus_register. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Edge Hub (optional pressure sensor), Modbus TCP integration. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `mstats` to query metrics indexes (pre-aggregated metric data).
• Pipeline stage (see **HVAC Duct Pressure & Velocity Monitoring**): delta static_press as press_delta
• `eval` defines or adjusts **filter_condition** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by duct_zone** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Filters the current rows with `where predominant_condition!="NORMAL"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.


Note: for mstats, confirm the metrics indexes in the search exist and your role can run mstats. This is separate from CIM data model acceleration.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm metric or event volume in the time range, then compare trends and device status to the Splunk Edge Hub admin UI or the mobile app for the same devices. Verify metric names, hosts, and time skew.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Duct pressure trend, filter condition gauge, maintenance alert timeline.
grandmaExplanation: We watch what edge sensors report from the physical world. We help you know if something is off with this area: hvac duct pressure & velocity before harm, damage, or wasted effort piles up.
dataModelAcceleration: Not applicable for CIM; ensure metrics index access and mstats permissions for the indexes in the search.
